title: Alone in Algiers: The activists fighting to preserve the Hirak movement's legacy
subhead: 'Resisting a repressive regime (3/3). Six years after the end of the pro-democracy movement that shook the country, judicial harassment continues to target those still seeking to promote freedom of expression.

'SpaceX stock market debut reads more like science fiction than a realistic business plan'
subhead: Elon Musk is basing the market valuation of his aerospace company, which is set to go public on the Nasdaq on Friday, on the promise of dominating technologies and markets that do not yet exist. In the space sector, however, the line between achievement and disaster is razor-thin, explains Le Monde editorialist Stéphane Lauer.

title: Spain must face a looming logistical headache ahead of August 12's total solar 'eclipse'
subhead: In two months, part of the country will watch the Sun disappear behind the Moon. Authorities, having only recently grasped the full implications of the event, are now scrambling to catch up.

title: Olise smashes hat-trick as France beats Northern Ireland in pre-World Cup game
subhead: Les Bleus scored a 3-1 World Cup warm-up win ahead of France's June 16 opener at MetLife Stadium in New Jersey against Senegal.

title: 'I am a libertarian and belong to no party': The octogenarian, his anarchist friends and the mystery of a bomb at McDonald's
subhead: In July 2025, an explosive device and a torn Israeli flag were left in front of a McDonald's in France. After a 10-month investigation, an 85-year-old former press photographer, who claims to be outraged by the war in Gaza, emerged as the main suspect, while a possible accomplice died by suicide on May 7, just before an anti-terrorism operation.

subhead: Projects such as Le Large, built on a historic Renault factory site on Seguin island, or at the Maison du Peuple in Paris's northwestern suburbs, illustrate how major corporations are taking over former industrial sites.

title: Women's desire, a men's issue?
subhead: No, women should not feel pressured to boost their libidos when they wane, writes sex columnist Maïa Mazaurette, who urges both men and women to reconsider female desire from a societal perspective.

title: Pope Leo XIV quotes Gandalf, highlighting Tolkien's close ties with Catholicism
subhead: A Lord of the Rings reference in the last papal encyclical has sparked a great deal of commentary. While it can be read as a response to the way Silicon Valley libertarians have appropriated Tolkien's writing, it also highlights the English author's religious convictions.
